key_insight: The paper shows that phase-field fracture can be solved end-to-end in PyTorch with matrix-free mechanics and differentiable implicit damage updates, enabling GPU-ready simulation and inverse analysis without custom compiled code.
engineering_relevance: This makes high-fidelity fracture simulation easier to integrate with optimization and machine-learning workflows while remaining portable across CPU and GPU platforms.
method: Element-wise tensor contractions and scatter accumulation replace global sparse stiffness assembly in the explicit dynamics loop.
An implicit, bound-constrained damage solve is differentiated via a custom backward rule using implicit differentiation through conjugate gradient iterations.
Supports AT1/AT2 regularization, multiple energy splits, and plane strain/plane stress settings.
evidence_signal: The solver was benchmarked on four dynamic and two quasi-static fracture cases and used for inverse recovery of fracture energy, achieving relative error below 10^-3 within a few accepted L-BFGS states.
watch_item: Performance and accuracy may depend on CG convergence, mesh size, and the chosen fracture decomposition, so robustness should be checked case by case.
paper_title: A matrix-free, differentiable PyTorch solver for phase-field fracture: Formulation, benchmarks, and inverse analysis
